title: Suteria fluminensis
taxonomy: Suteria fluminensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Suteria, which is a group of plants found in South America. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family includes over 5,000 species of plants, many of which are vines or shrubs. Suteria fluminensis is classified as a shrub-like plant native to Brazil.
uses: Suteria fluminensis has been found to have various medicinal properties. Suteria fluminensis is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Brazil, where it is valued for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is also used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots are often used in folk medicine to make teas and infusions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential as a medicinal plant.

intro: Suteria fluminensis, also known as Rio de Janeiro Suteria, is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its purported health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Suteria fluminensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
